summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Right elbow pain radiating to the neck.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports ongoing right elbow pain, which has been present for an unspecified duration. The pain radiates up to the neck and chest. He describes the pain as aching and occasionally sharp. He has not engaged in any activities that he believes may have caused the pain. He reports taking occasional ibuprofen for the pain and notes it provides some relief. The patient reports intermittent blood sugar fluctuations and has been taking metformin 500mg daily and norvasc 2.5mg daily. He denies recent diarrhea or bowel issues, mentioning a concern about Joe biting and his inability to stop pooping.

Findings
Blood pressure was 150/90 mmHg. The patient reports experiencing chest pain, shortness of breath, and a slight murmur heard upon cardiac auscultation. An electrocardiogram is ordered to evaluate for possible cardiac ischemia.

Assessment
The patient presents with chest pain and elevated blood pressure, raising concern for possible cardiac ischemia. His history of hypertension and diabetes requires ongoing management.

Plan
Order an electrocardiogram and a glucose test. Administer aspirin and nitroglycerin as needed for chest pain. Continue metformin 500mg daily and norvasc 2.5mg daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in 24-48 hours to assess the results of the electrocardiogram and glucose test and to determine the next course of action. If indicated, arrange for transport to the hospital for further evaluation and treatment. } /> ? 500 mg daily and norvasc 2.5 mg daily. reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Left arm pain.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Zachary Wood is a 45-year-old right-hand-dominant male who presents to the clinic today for the evaluation of left arm pain. The onset of his pain began yesterday when he woke up with pain. He denies any specific injury. The patient locates his pain from his left elbow up all the way to his neck. He describes his pain as an achiness. The patient has been taking Motrin for pain, which does not provide him with relief. He also reports intermittent chest pain that radiates up his neck. He notes that he had to stop cutting the grass the other day because of chest pain. The patient denies any shortness of breath.

For his hypertension, the patient states that his blood pressure has been around 125/72 recently. He is still taking 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

Regarding his type 2 diabetes, his last hemoglobin A1c was 7.2. He takes metformin 500 mg. The patient states that he does not check his blood glucose levels regularly.

MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient reports that he has a past medical history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes.

MEDICATIONS

Patient reports that he currently takes metformin 500 mg and Norvasc 2.5 mg.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Reports chest pain.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath.
Musculoskeletal: Reports left elbow, arm, and neck pain.

VITALS

The patient's blood pressure today is 150/90.

PHYSICAL EXAM

CV: Slight murmur detected, not heard before.

ASSESSMENT

Left arm pain.
Type 2 diabetes.
Hypertension.

PLAN

Regarding his left arm pain, I have explained to him that his symptoms are likely a mild heart attack. I have recommended that we obtain an EKG for further evaluation. I have also recommended that the patient take aspirin and nitroglycerin for pain. The patient will follow up with me once the EKG results are available for review and further discussion.

In regards to his diabetes, the patient will continue the 500 mg of metformin for now.

For his hypertension, the patient will continue the 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports experiencing chest pain, shortness of breath, and a slight murmur heard upon cardiac auscultation.
section_miscategorizations.expected_section: Mixed (HPI for symptoms / Findings for murmur)
section_miscategorizations.reason: Patient-reported symptoms (chest pain, shortness of breath) belong in HPI, while the murmur is an exam finding. Grouping them together in Findings blurs the distinction between subjective symptoms and objective exam, which could mislead a reader about what was actually observed on exam versus reported by the patient.

section_miscategorizations.text: An electrocardiogram is ordered to evaluate for possible cardiac ischemia.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an order/diagnostic plan, not an existing result or exam finding. Placing it in Findings misrepresents it as something already performed or resulted rather than a planned test.
